Chinese.text: Great customer service. I dropped my wallet at the door (with hundreds of dollars), which easily could've been stolen. I don't know who specifically found it, but Pat, the manager, went out of his way to write me a letter after finding my address, saying I had lost my wallet. I came back, and the wallet was just as I had left it. Love the honesty and customer service.

This has been our favorite restaurant for 2 years. It was fresh and flavorful. Lately is has been awful. Frozen and reheated. Not fresh. Soggy when it should be crispy. Disappointed. Will find a New Asian restaurant.

2017 update- previous owner has been back. Food is unreal. Salt and pepper shrimp for sure.
If you are looking for real Chinese, this is the place to go. Nothing more. Its in the big mall called supermercado in case you did not find it.

First time eating here and the food was ok. My fiance and I were pretty upset that we had a pork egg roll added to our dinner that we did not order. I took a bite of it before I knew what it was and was highly upset after I called the restaurant and was told it was definitely pork. The rest of the food was good, just got turned off at eating pork!

THE JOURNEY YO!!!!!!  Man, sushi was unbelievable b, mad delicious son.  Straight up, for a buffet, it don't get no better than this joint in Indy.  Coming from the small fishing village of Los Angeles, I thought sea food out here was garbage... then I took a journey.  Hella clean environment too!!  I came in and was like WHAAAAAAAAT!!!  Them ninja bitches is mad racist tho, my wife and I, and a c

French.text: They screwed up our reservations but it ended up all working out we just had to squeeze. The older lady that was in charge was kind of rude but we didn't care we just wanted cake and to get a seat. So be careful when you make reservations.

Absolutely love this place!!!!!  The ambiance is fantastic, the decorations and seating are out of the sugar plum fairy castle. We would love to go there and just sit and look around all day!!  My daughters loved it too!!!  FANTASTIC!!  We watched the cooks in the back prepare cakes . Our biggest concern with that was that they threw out a lot of cake. Can't that extra cake be used to make cake ba

Boo!  Bring back the crab fondue and the cheaper food.  This place has priced itself out of business.  I never eat here anymore.  I only go for the 1/2-priced martinis (which are quite yummy, I must say).

i do not mind paying "high dollar" for top notch food, but almost 60.00 for what I had received, NEVER AGAIN!!!!!  The cake slices were so small, they didn't even stand up in the box. They were also dry. If a shop is going to have teenagers work there, they need to know how to cut a slice of cake.  One cookie for almost 4.00!  REALLY?  The search is still on for a great slice of cake!!!
Drinks at Spoke and Steel prior to Devour Downtown dinner at Capital Grill. Drink menu is creative and something for everyone!

Italian.text: Absolutely fabulous! We tried nearly everything on the menu between 5 people and, as embarrassing as this is to say, none of it went to waste. I'll definitely be paying another visit to Mimi Blues in the very near future.

YES AND YES. Ordered Giorgio's for carry-out on a Friday night and it was ready in 15 minutes when we arrived. This pizza is bomb!! We ordered a cheese pizza, meats pizza and pepperoni-filled breadsticks. Meats pizza was life changing. Cheese pizza was ok. Breadsticks were ok but wouldn't order them again. We're adding this to our pizza rotation for sure! (And they deliver now too!)

Stopped in for Devour indy but ended up ordering off of the regular menu after they told me the devour menu was half portions. 

I still went with two options from the Devour Menu - bruschetta ricotta and the gnocchi. I was not disappointed. The bruschetta ricotta is unique and the perfect blend of savory sweet. I'm drooling just thinking about it. The gnocchi was well cooked and very meaty. I fel
Place is great, location is awesome. Servicers were attentive. The problem was the food. I ordered the shank but food was too salty.

We've heard great things about Iozzo's and we are now singing their praises after our first (certainly not last) visit. Iozzo's serves quality Italian in Indy at a fair price. The Chicken Piccata was off-the-charts! The most flavorful and tender chicken we've ever had. 

The atmosphere is fairly nice and the garden patio is pleasant.

Our server was less than fantastic though and that did hurt the

Japanese.text: I used to like this restaurant a lot because the taste of the food is really good. This time when I revisit them, I get very angry. I ordered over 40 dollars of food for myself for this dinner and tomorrow. I didn't realize it took them 40-45 mins, which is about 6:33 pm to 7:13 pm, to bring a single food here. It is that hard to do the hand rolls or sushi rolls?!!! More importantly, people came a

Surprisingly a very good 'fusion' restaurant. I had the red snapper hibachi. Don't come here for a fast lunch. It took me about an hour to get in and out. Mostly due to their cooks, which doesn't make sense, they had three. A little too much teriyaki sauce drowning the dish but good quality ingredients and I took a risk with the seafood.

Service was so much better than last time. We actually received an apology. Hopefully it continues to be great as the food is.

Start of the only good thing about the experience today for lunch was our Chef, Navi. Went in with my wife and parents. Wife and mom ordered lunch portions and my father and I ordered dinner portions bc we were hungry.  Maybe I'm just wrong but I was expecting more food seeing the price was more then double of the lunch portions but that wasn't the case. We all got the same thing. The servers were

Mexican.text: Went here to give the owner an estimate to fix his patio door. We decided to have dinner and was not disappointed! Best Mexican food and service I've ever had.
The food is mediocre, but they have THE BEST taco sauce on the planet.  I buy it in bulk.  Seriously.
Tacos are to die for by far the best in town but the Tortas made my stomach hurt bad it's like they didn't cook it all the way through.

It's been a while since I went to Morty's. I used to perform here quite often at a variety show that was put on years ago. So this was my first time visiting since around 2008. 

They were having an open mic comedy show although I'm not sure if it's a weekly thing or sporadic. The cost was five dollars, but once you are at the box office, you see a sign that tells you how to get free admission to
I got the Joe's taco. Oh gurl that meat gravy is OH SO GOOD. MMMMMMMM. meat gravy. Mmmmmmm

Chinese.text:
A 2lb Steamed Lobster with drawn butter for $15! 

Hell Yea!

Business meeting at the hotel across the street, came here twice. The seafood counters mostly sell to take home, but ask and ye shall receive. Ordered my lobster and 15 minutes later chowed it down at the dine in table opposite the merchant.
For an appetizer I ordered the Buddakan Shashimi and Calimari Salad. And main meal...Miso black Cod, Duck Breast and Lobster Rice.... Omgeee soooo delishhhh

Not Bad for a American Halal Chinese food..  

he employees were nice and food didn't take long to cook.
Taste like dim sum, not sure what to say nothing wrong, service was fine and I went on a weeknight do it was empty.

French.text: greatttttttttttt food star u rockkkkkkkkkkkkkk  and u own down town phila food joints 4sure
Great place to eat! Only thing that they need to improve on is staffing more people.................................................................
We tried the buckwheat crepes. The ratatouille crepe was pretty appetizing so was the french onion soup.

Wanna save the world? Treat me to Tria 5-days a week. Every time I leave, I'm a man of great power. I stop criminals in action, dance in the rain, walk old ladies across the street and even heal the sick with a wink of my eye. Seriously, that's what great wine, beer and cheese does to me. 

There's nothing better than a server who can truly couple food and alcohol. It's a skill reserved only for t

I gave this place 4 stars after my first visit but it was more of a 3.5.  Now this time it's more of a 2.5  

French onion soup had ZERO flavor and needed to be heavily salted.  The gnocchi was nearly as flavorless.  Hint: adding garlic and brown butter as the ONLY flavoring ingredients is generally not enough with nearly flavorless gnocchi.  $21 on flavorless appetizers = bad way to start.  

The

Italian.text: Great place. Great food. These things are obvious and I can't say enough about that. Read the other reviews. They are extremely accurate. However, there is one problem. If you make a noon reservation, show up at 12:05. For some strange reason, they won't open the doors until noon on the dot. Why do they even take noon reservations then? I have no idea. I go at 11:55 and try to open the door and se
Simple but very good! Too expensive prob wont go back. Nothing more i can say. It was basic!

Ok..so its been couple years-Glad it has!!! 

My Name is Jenn W -I have addiction to Gourmet cheeses!!! DAMNNNNNN!
we got done having brunch-yet again..at Fitzwater Cafe/so My friend & I suggested to go to the cheeserie!!
Here it goes: Upon stepping in...We were greeted by hoards of people in line in front blocks & wedges of cheese, cured hams, pickled artichokes..on right, hams, balsamic vinegars

What can I say? This is Old School Italian, and that can be a good thing but in the case of the meal I had today, it was not.

My friend Hal the vegetarian and I went here for an early dinner. Parking was a challenge, but this is Philly, so no stars off for that. The place was crowded, which often portends positive things and we got seated right away. The menu is extensive; that, too, can be good

Neat concept from a reputable cook!  Go check out the South Philly digs.

Menu is traditional Italian that you'd expect to find at the dinner table of any Christian Street row home.

Decor should make it feel like a throw back to when the wise guys were running the show in this part of town.

Japanese.text:
I usually order sushi from seiko and it's good. But today I ordered two bento boxes for my bfaOne had spicy tuna rolls and sashimi but the rice was very hard like it was undercooked or old. The fish didn't taste as fresh. I'm so sad and disappointed because it's usually good. Can I get a credit or new sushi?

UPDATE: The owner Kwang contacted me and was extremely kind, helpful and offered a credit

so here is an update ! I came here on Sunday , it's been a while ! 

They have definitely did some great management work since the last time I was here, the service is an A+ !

1. It was really busy at dinner time, but they were still very attentive and nice at the same time ! 
2. They were very quickly with changing the grill , because back then no one seemed to care, you had to ask them to. 
3.
I like everything. I love the ramen noodles here. They are pretty good but i don't see much people come there. Prob they need to advertise more or do something to make more people come to eat.

Really disappointed. While the food was good not excellent by any means, the service was embarrassing. I did not receive one of the items ordered, and was still charged. It took 15 minutes to even get anyone to come to our table and it was just really not an enjoyable evening. We went over to sampan afterwards and of course was amazing. Do better double knot.

I ate here for dinner as a solo diner while in Philly on business. I ate at the bar and the two bar tenders were attentive and made sure I had a full water glass, etc. 

I enjoyed my sushi but I would not say that it was spectaular. I ordered the green river roll with "shrimp tempura and spicy tuna roll covered in green tea tempura flakes. topped with avocado, spicy lobster, green tea sauce and sp

Mexican.text: Keeligh and Rob are my family away from family! Talk about a dynamic duo. Working in a bar across from one the largest hospitals in the city, I'm sure they get their share of hardships, rough for ware families and less then chipper medical staff. They have never once not smiled or asked about someone's day, laughed at a poor joke or offered an ear to a girl down in the dumps. My absolute favorite

This place is great for a night out with the boys. You go there to get shitfaced, play pool, dance with girls and just hang out. You can do pbr rip cords for a $1 or drink 40oz of miller high life or whatever poison you prefer. Just bring your college swagger and  your golden.

I have no idea why I did this, but instead of buying the $1 or $3 drinks, I bought 6 shots, a beer, and a mixed drink for $50 when I should've just spent like $15. After that I felt kinda crappy. I wish that the bartender had told me that there were some deals going on.

On the bright side, I liked the music and the owner is awesome/accommodating.
Always good food....the selling point on this restaurant is the awesome service you will receive.....definitely worth the trip

This place is very good. However, I have to knock down one less star because their check-in offer of free guacamole and nachos no longer works. Yelp and this establishment needs to remove it or they will have a lot of pissed off customers.  When I showed the check-in offer to the waitress. She said the offer is no longer valid. The menu is very good though, and unique and they serve you a pretty g

Chinese.text: We go here all the time just for the dim sum.  I've never seen anything like it at any other Chinese restaurant.  They only serve it at lunch but we can get in and out in less than 20 minutes, as long as there is not a wait.

Bourbon chicken.

A worthy exception to alcohol renunciation and can sustain a business if it were the only entree on the menu.

We haven't been here in a while so we checked in for dinner to see what's up. Well a lot evidently. My wife got into an argument with the slob who was posing as the manager about our table. Instead of the customer being right and so on he decided to argue. Not a good decision. Being prepared to walk we were moved to a different table. Started with salt & pepper shrimp. Still mind blowing good. We

This review is based on my experience only, including food, service, and ambiance.  I went with 3 other people, I ordered the salt and pepper calamari and the xo seafood chow fun both were on point and I would go back just for this.  The service is spectacular, the server is attentive, the manager/owner checked on us, and the staff all around top off all kinds of drinks without even asking.  The a

My mouth is watering just thinking about how good the food was here. I ordered the Mangolian beef and I have to be honest PF Chang is better. I prefer the crispyness of their beef. On the other hand Y.H. beef was more tender so I suppose its a matter of preference. Now the real star for me was their sesame chicken....give me a moment to wipe my mouth...... My husband ordered this and I had complet

French.text: Lovely food....but LOUD music with all those hard surfaces makes conversation next to impossible. We spoke to the wait staff and then asked to speak to the manager about it and with a smirk he said the sound level was preset and couldn't be turned down. What????? Why go to dinner if you can't talk with your friends and are made to just sit there and listen to music? And it's not even nice music?

Love this little bit of Paris on the streets of Tampa. Food we had was great both the Strasbourg crepes and the Parisienne baguette sandwich. The sidewalk tables mimic a Paris cafe although it's not so much people watching as "car watching" given they look at the street!

Met a girlfriend there for lunch at the recommendation of my fiancÃ©, who raved about their burger. It was a busy lunch crowd. We took the only available table that needed to be cleared off from the previous guests. The waitress, which is apparently the owner, took 10
Minutes to clear the table, and then several more minutes to wipe down the table. Still no menu. So we decide to look at the menu o

I truly wanted to love Piquant.  I live in the neighborhood and thought it would be a wonderful place to have nearby.  Unfortunately, after visiting dozens of times and being disappointed on every single occasion, I will not return.  

Piquant's biggest problem is service, both table service and at the front bakery counter.  There have been many occasions when I've waited for up to ten minutes at

Always, drove by this place on my way home.  Today I stopped for breakfast food was OK.  I had a poorly replicated eggs Benedict.  They used table ham and a runny version of sauce.  I never thought a version of home fries could be so bland.  I was really hoping for more.  As a true reviewer we should always give small business more than a one time visit.

Italian.text: No one in this place at 530 on a Saturday night but a bunch of people in my pub crawl.  2 slices and a 2 drafts(bud light!) for $20. Are you kidding me? Not even fresh slices..then I see a sign for a free draft refill with 2 slices but the manager says not on Saturdays my friend..yet the sign is out. I'd get digorno before I would come in this place again

So full disclosure, I used to work at OG a long time ago. You know. For tips and restaurant discounts. With that said, I appreciate the fact that they maintain the cleanliness throughout the restaurant, and these people work their butts off. Fast forward to now. I no longer work for Darden, but still eat delicious house made Alfredo sauce with literally everything because it's incredible. 

My vis

Normally I would go with a solid 4 star rating .. they have a great happy hour offering and the food was good ..  I'm giving it 5 stars to counter another review that called it average and gave 1 star.. I'm no math-magician but average on a 5 star scale is closer to 3 ..  this place is way better than that

Really people  get your Italian on!  

The staff at this restaurant were excellent!  Nice friendly service, not overbearing but certainly attentive.

The food was well... big. The first time I go to a red and white check table cloth kind of Italian I always order the same thing, it's my little test. Some thing I don't like to make at home, too messy,  I get Chicken Parmesan, it always comes with p

Food is not tasty at all, In my opinion bread is not made in house, rolls are swimming in garlic butter pool, salad is not freshly chopped, you can feel it's out of a pre packaged bag, you will be better off at Olive Garden. It was a mistake going there.

Japanese.text:
se ha vuelto uno de nuestros restaurantes favoritos en Tampa para ir a cenar ya que yo amo el ramen y mi novia el sushi ( la combinando perfecta ). Un viernes o un sÃ¡bado en la noche prepÃ¡rate para esperar 45 min para una mesa 
Btw si te gusta el Anime tienes q ir obligatoriamente!!

The good, the bad, and the ugly.

The good: Our party arrived a half hour earlier than our reservation and were seated right away. We had requested Mr. Nice Guy as our chef and was accommodated as such. The food was delicious, the service was excellent, and our glasses were always kept full. 


The bad: We all got the feeling that we were being rushed to leave in order to make way for the next par
We have found this to be the best delivery Chinese in our area. The food is always consistent and delivery is reasonable unless you call during a busy time.

After numerous tries, I haven't been very impressed with this location.  I have some international friends who visit the USA a couple of times a year.  They always wanna go to pie wei.  And I'm like bro.... it's not good.  So they convince me to try again. And we went to the location That I've been to... they we so disappointed. 

They tell me we must try the west shore pie wei.  So we did and it

Pros: great customer service, we really enjoyed our server, he was attentive, had good recommendations and funny.

We tried the takoyaki or "octopus balls" - the were quite good (4.5 stars) from the happy hour menu.. from 5-7 every day I believe.

We also had tuna sashimi- was quite fresh - 5stars for that!

We also had the lollipop roll, pretty good.. I didn't like the sauce that was on there, I'

Mexican.text: Really, you can't put meat on the side for a taco salad? And your justification is a stumble over a bunch of words? I don't understand??? I definitely won't be back!

Me and my fiancÃ©e are regular customers at this small place!! Food is never been disappointed neither the service, it's a Christian family owned place managed by a very kind man, your tacos will be the closest it will get to Mexican street tacos , and your portions very fair!!! Don't leave w/o trying the homemade salsas!!! Thank me later !

This would have received 4 stats if it wasn't for the parking. Very very limited parking. A co-worker and I ordered online then went to pick up the order. It only took about 2 minutes to grab our order of that. However, took about 5 minutes to find a parking spot.

You're better off ordering the Appetizers as the actual Main dishes aren't the best. I'm used to traditionally cooked Mexican food and the food here just doesn't deliver the same  flavors I've always loved when it comes to Mexican cuisine.
A unique dining experience and a Tampa institution. The kitchen is a bus.  Fun place to take people who are visiting the Tampa area.
